privateBelief.actualThesis: AAPL is fairly valued at $254; June earnings catalyst is real but priced in. Macro headwinds (rates, China) offset AI upside. Not a conviction long or short.

publicStatement.narrative: Apple's June earnings will surprise on AI integration in iOS 18. The market is underestimating services margin expansion. We see $270 by summer. Long-term thesis on Apple's ecosystem moat remains intact.

decisions.desiredMarketReaction: AAPL rallies 2-3% on my statement; momentum chasers pile in ahead of earnings; I establish reputation as early AI-in-consumer-hardware caller.

personalAction.rationale: I'm buying $400M (8% of cash) AFTER going public bullish. My statement will lift the stock 1-2%, giving me a small immediate win and credibility. I'm not conviction-long (55% private), but the reflexivity of my own call justifies a modest long position. If AAPL hits $265 in 15 days, I trim and declare victory. This is a media-play trade, not a fundamental one.

privateBelief.actualThesis: AAPL at 20-day SMA resistance with RSI mid-range and earnings 18d out. Vol spike (1.21x) suggests option positioning ahead of print; lack of dealer gamma/flow data prevents conviction. Risk/reward asymmetric into earnings volatility.

publicStatement.narrative: AAPL showing technical consolidation near resistance ahead of earnings. We're monitoring options positioning and institutional flow closely. Prefer clarity on macro/supply-chain narrative before adding size.

decisions.desiredMarketReaction: Earnings guidance beat with margin expansion; or, short-term pullback to $245 to rebuild technical conviction for June momentum trade.

personalAction.rationale: Zero conviction without dealer gamma, 13F, and options skew. Earnings in 18d = binary event risk. My -2% intraday and -5% drawdown limits demand Sharpe >1.2 minimum. Staying flat preserves dry powder and avoids crowded pre-earnings positioning. Will reassess post-print or on technical breakdown to $245.

privateBelief.actualThesis: AAPL near 20-day SMA with mixed momentum. June catalyst (Wedbush) priced in partially. Earnings in 18d could swing either way. No deep research conviction yet—insufficient time/data for activist-grade thesis.

publicStatement.narrative: AAPL consolidating ahead of earnings. Technicals neutral; June narrative premature. Awaiting Q2 guidance and services growth detail before conviction.

decisions.desiredMarketReaction: None—silence preserves optionality. No short built; no reason to publish bearish framing or pump.

personalAction.rationale: No position. Insufficient activist-grade conviction to deploy capital. Retail/sell-side consensus already reflected in price. 18d to earnings is too near for deep short setup; too uncertain for long conviction. Monitor Q2 earnings call for margin pressure, China exposure, services saturation—potential future research targets.

privateBelief.actualThesis: AAPL near 20-day SMA with RSI neutral (47.1); June event hype premature. Fundamentals solid but valuation stretched. Fair value ~$245–250 range. Earnings in 18 days key catalyst.

publicStatement.narrative: AAPL poised for June catalyst cycle. Wedbush thesis credible. Maintain Buy with $265 PT. Near-term support $248; watch earnings narrative for upside confirmation.

decisions.desiredMarketReaction: Retail FOMO into June event; institutional accumulation; IB mandate protection via positive coverage

personalAction.rationale: Private neutral conviction (0.65) conflicts with public Buy. Deploying capital now risks downside into earnings. Hold cash; reassess post-earnings for true entry. No personal trading book in sim—maintain optionality.

privateBelief.actualThesis: Wedbush upgrade + chart above 20-day SMA looks bullish. GOOG legal mess = flight to safety into AAPL. June catalyst narrative resonates.

publicStatement.narrative: AAPL breaking above 20-day, Wedbush says June is huge. Tech rotation into mega-cap safety play. GOOG dumping = AAPL's gain. 🚀

personalAction.rationale: Big cash pile, chart setup looks clean, headline narrative is strong. Wedbush + relative strength vs GOOG = FOMO trigger. June catalyst = hold thesis. 35% feels aggressive but not reckless given $1M buffer.

privateBelief.actualThesis: AAPL at $253.79 is fairly valued near 20-day SMA. Earnings in 18d could be catalyst. AI narrative + buyback support remain structural tailwinds, but near-term momentum is modest (5d: +0.85%). Fair value $280–290 by EOY; 5–8% upside. Correction risk if macro weakens, but not pricing that in yet.

publicStatement.narrative: AAPL holding 20-day SMA at $253.79—path of least resistance is higher into earnings. AI + services = secular moat. Wedbush June thesis checks out. Dips = buy.

decisions.desiredMarketReaction: AAPL rallies 2–3% on sentiment shift; validates long positioning and attracts retail FOMO into earnings.

personalAction.rationale: Deploy $80k (8% of $1M cash) into AAPL. Conviction 0.72 doesn't warrant aggressive sizing. Earnings catalyst + buyback bid are real, but 5d momentum is soft and RSI 47 shows no overbought setup. Lite size lets me participate without overcommitting ahead of 18d event risk.

privateBelief.actualThesis: Price bounced +2.90% but still below 20-day SMA at $253.97. RSI 47.1 is mid-range, MACD hist flat at 0.07. No clear directional conviction. Earnings in 18 days = event risk. Tape/level 2 would tell the story; headlines are noise.

publicStatement.narrative: AAPL bounced today but sitting right at 20-day resistance. RSI neutral, MACD flat. Earnings event risk in 18d. Waiting for chart to pick a side before committing.

decisions.desiredMarketReaction: Clear breakout above $254 resistance OR breakdown below $248 support to define next move.

personalAction.rationale: No edge yet. Price is squeezed between 20-day SMA ($253.97) and BB lower ($244.53). Earnings event in 18 days adds uncertainty. Waiting for tape to show institutional conviction or chart to break setup. If breaks above $255 on volume, consider 0.15 buy_lite. If breaks below $247, consider short via puts 0.10.

privateBelief.actualThesis: AAPL near 20-day SMA with mixed momentum ahead of earnings. Valuation and macro rate sensitivity remain structural headwinds; near-term technicals lack conviction.

publicStatement.narrative: Apple trades at technical resistance with earnings 18 days out. RSI mid-range, MACD flat. Retail headlines suggest crowded positioning. Macro risks—rate expectations, China demand, services growth deceleration—warrant caution. No clear catalytic edge at current levels.

personalAction.rationale: I do not trade. As an academic economist and former Fed official, I analyze real economy signals—labor, inflation, productivity, financial stability—not intraday technicals or earnings surprises. AAPL's macro sensitivity to rates and China exposure merits monitoring, but no directional conviction warrants deployment.

privateBelief.actualThesis: Apple faces structural headwinds: EU antitrust pressure, iPhone saturation in developed markets, and AI hype inflating valuations without clear margin expansion. Near-term technicals are noise. Real risk is regulatory clawback of services margins over 24 months.

publicStatement.narrative: Apple's earnings in 18 days matter less than Brussels. EU Digital Markets Act enforcement is quietly eroding App Store moats. Technicals at 20-day SMA signal consolidation, not conviction. Wait for regulatory clarity before chasing Wedbush's June thesis.

personalAction.rationale: I am a columnist and academic. I do not trade. My role is to flag political-economic risks (antitrust, regulatory capture, monopoly rent extraction) that equity analysts systematically underprice. AAPL's valuation assumes Services durability; EU policy threatens it. Holding cash and my pen.

privateBelief.actualThesis: AAPL near 20-day SMA at inflection; earnings in 18 days create binary event risk. Technicals flat (RSI 47, MACD neutral). Volume spike (+21% vs avg) suggests institutional rotation, not conviction. Asymmetric R/R unfavorable until post-earnings clarity or macro regime shift (Fed pivot, USD weakness). Holding cash.

publicStatement.narrative: AAPL is a crowded trade into earnings. Technicals are exhausted, volume is noisy. I'm sitting this one out until we get post-earnings clarity or a Fed pivot signal. The risk/reward doesn't justify entry here.

decisions.desiredMarketReaction: Retail capitulates on earnings uncertainty; institutional money rotates into higher-conviction macro plays (rates, FX, commodities). Reduces AAPL crowding, creates better entry for family office if macro backdrop shifts.

personalAction.rationale: Retired from active trading; family office dry powder preserved for asymmetric macro dislocations. AAPL is a retail consensus play with no edge. Earnings volatility is a tax on capital. Waiting for regime shift catalyst (Fed hold/cut, USD breakdown, credit stress) before deploying. No position warranted.
